overview.description: AI-powered legal research for Czech and EU law with verified citations
overview.description_long: DirectCase is an AI legal research assistant that searches 1.4 million sources of Czech and European law — including Czech court judgments, EU regulations, ECHR and CJEU rulings — and returns answers with full, verifiable citations. Designed for lawyers and judges, it explicitly prohibits hallucination by grounding every response in its closed legal database. Previously known as Lawly.

history.origin_story: Pavol Trávnik, a lawyer and AI engineer, repeatedly encountered the pain of hours-long manual case law research during litigation. He built DirectCase (originally 'Lawly') to let machines do the searching so lawyers can focus on expert judgment. Radovan Oprendek joined as co-founder and CEO to lead product and go-to-market.

key_milestones.event: Product started under Determinant s.r.o. (founded May 16, 2023)
CendAI pilot at Prague High Court — 12 judges and court assistants tested DirectCase, finding 50-minute time savings per research session
DirectCase s.r.o. incorporated as dedicated legal entity (January 8, 2026)

notable_clients: Vrchní soud v Praze (Prague High Court)
partnerships: CendAI (Centrum pro digitalizaci a umělou inteligenci v justici)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: DirectCase maintains a curated, closed Czech legal database of 1.4 million sources with a proprietary citation-verification layer — this is harder for general-purpose LLMs to replicate out of the box. However, larger legal tech platforms or well-funded competitors could build similar RAG pipelines over the same public Czech judicial databases.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: The legal research market in Czech Republic is a clear, proven pain point with paying professional customers. CendAI validation at the Prague High Court confirms real demand. Traditional legal databases (Beck Online, ASPI) are incumbents but lack AI-native search, leaving room for disruption.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No external funding identified — the company appears bootstrapped with two co-founders and a small team. The legal entity was only formally incorporated in January 2026, and there is no visible runway or investor backing to weather a competitive threat or slow growth period.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Strong product validation (CendAI pilot, active users including judges) and a domain-specific database moat partially offset the high funding risk. The company is very early stage but operates in a well-defined, regulated vertical with sticky professional users who value verified citations over generic AI.

metadata.notes: Company originally operated under 'Determinant s.r.o.' (ICO 19345861, founded May 2023). New legal entity 'DirectCase s.r.o.' (ICO 24337269) incorporated January 2026 with both co-founders as equal 50% shareholders. Product was previously called 'Lawly' before rebranding to DirectCase. Radovan Oprendek's email is radovan.oprendek@directcase.ai. The company address on CNAIP (Holečkova 836/69, Praha 5) belongs to Determinant s.r.o.; the new entity is at Zenklova 2530/23, Praha 8.
